skill.name: reviewing-code
skill.description: Guides structured code reviews with project checks, local validation commands, and clear approval decisions.
skill.summary: Review code changes with consistent findings, checks, and verdicts.
skill.author: BPSAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell command snippets and weak cryptography patterns. The weak cryptography hits are false positives from ordinary markdown text, while the command snippets are local review helper commands. The skill is publishable with a low external command risk note.

low_findings.title: Local Review Commands Suggested
low_findings.description: The skill documents local commands for validation, git diff review, grep checks, and TODO searches. These commands are legitimate for code review but should run only in a trusted repository because they inspect local files and invoke project tooling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in bash code fences and are scoped to local validation, git diff, and grep checks. I found no evidence of remote execution, downloads, credential access, or command construction from untrusted input.

low_findings.title: xargs Use In TODO Search
low_findings.description: The TODO search pipes changed file names into xargs grep. The command is intended for local review, but unusual file names could affect grep arguments if run without additional safeguards.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The xargs invocation is directly present and uses a fixed grep command, so arbitrary shell execution is not indicated. The remaining concern is limited to command argument handling with unusual file names.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings correspond to ordinary prose and markdown review template text. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, cipher usage, or security implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain a description field and a markdown heading placeholder. I found no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic usage in executable code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fence Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Several Ruby or shell backtick detections map to markdown code fence delimiters or a review output example. They do not perform shell execution by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported lines are markdown fence markers or non-executable formatting examples. The semantic context is instructional markdown, not Ruby code or runtime command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes bash snippets that an AI assistant may choose to run during review. The commands are local and review-oriented, but external command use remains a risk factor for community skills.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The bash snippets are explicit and include project tooling plus git and grep commands. They do not show malicious intent, but they are still actionable external commands.

content.user_title: Review Code Changes With Structured Feedback
content.value_statement: Code reviews often miss consistency, tests, or project-specific checks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a clear review workflow.
actual_capabilities: Runs or suggests local validation commands for project checks.
Reviews git diffs against the main branch.
Organizes feedback into must fix, should fix, and consider sections.
Checks for tests, type hints, docstrings, and hardcoded values.
Searches changed code for debug statements, TODOs, and possible secrets.
Produces an approval, comment, or request changes verdict.
limitations: It depends on the local project having the referenced bpsai-pair commands.
It does not replace human judgment for security, architecture, or product risk.
It reviews available diffs and cannot assess code that is not present locally.
Command snippets should be run only in repositories the user trusts.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Gatekeeping
use_cases.description: Review a feature branch before merge and identify required fixes with clear file references.

use_cases.title: Self Review Before Opening a PR
use_cases.description: Check changed code for tests, debug statements, TODOs, and project conventions before asking for review.

use_cases.title: Team Review Standardization
use_cases.description: Apply the same review sections and verdict language across different reviewers and repositories.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Diff Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of current branch changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current branch against main. Use must fix, should fix, consider, positive notes, and verdict sections.

prompt_templates.title: Pre-PR Quality Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer wants a final pass before opening a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the available local checks, inspect the git diff, and tell me what I should fix before creating a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Project Convention Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review should focus on repository standards.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these changes for type hints, docstrings, tests, hardcoded values, mocked external services, and consistency with existing patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Release-Critical Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a strict review of this branch. Prioritize correctness, missing tests, hidden regressions, unsafe external service usage, and merge readiness.

output_examples.input: Review a branch that adds a new service client without tests.
output: Must Fix: Add tests for success, failure, and retry behavior before merge.
Should Fix: Move the service endpoint into configuration instead of hardcoding it.

output_examples.input: Review a small documentation and type hint update.
output: Summary: The change is narrow and follows the existing style.
Positive Notes: Public functions now have clearer signatures and docstrings.

output_examples.input: Check a branch for debugging artifacts and secrets.
output: Must Fix: Remove the debug breakpoint before merge.
Should Fix: Replace the sample token value with a documented placeholder.

best_practices: Run validation commands before writing the final review.
Cite specific files and lines for every required fix.
Separate blocking defects from optional improvements.
anti_patterns: Do not approve changes without checking tests and changed files.
Do not mix style preferences with required correctness fixes.
Do not run command snippets in untrusted repositories.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews code changes, project checks, test coverage, debug artifacts, TODOs, and possible secret strings.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: It provides command guidance. The assistant may run commands when the environment and user permissions allow it.

faq.question: Can it review pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to inspect branch diffs and produce structured pull request feedback.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it a security scanner?
faq.answer: No. It can notice risky patterns, but it is a general code review workflow.

faq.question: What should users verify manually?
faq.answer: Users should verify architectural risk, business logic, security impact, and whether local commands are safe to run.
